Document:
# Rule 3: Metagaming and Metacommunication Acting on information your character would not have, or providing unknown information to another player is considered metagaming. If you are in a voice call with other players, you are still expected to relay information through IC channels (talking, radio, etc.) to preserve in-game roleplay. IC information should not be posted in any public area where other parties can gain an advantage. Any form of gaining an advantage through metacommunication will result in a ban. All public Discord channels should refrain from discussing any ongoing round information until the round ends.
## Metagaming Examples
## NOT Metagaming: - Xenomorphs destroying colony assets before marines land, such as lights, vending machines, etc. - Xenomorphs and Marines setting traps to catch and kill the opposing side. - Marines killing themselves after being infected or captured by Xenomorphs.
## Examples of Metagaming: - Xenomorphs sitting next to the dropship landing zone and attacking the moment the marines open the doors. - Xenomorphs melting every wall of the colony in a specific location to destroy a building/defense point. - Xenomorphs walling off marine bodies with the primary purpose of preventing revivals. - Killing or otherwise preventing monkeys from being captured as facehugger hosts prior to the marines landing.
## Metacommunication Examples
## NOT Metacommunication: - Teaching the game and various mechanics through voice chat, LOOC, or other out-of-game communication methods. - Streaming the game without delays.
## Examples of Metacommunication: - Silently following a friend without any IC communication or interaction. - Telling someone the location of an enemy, event details, or other character information that would otherwise be unavailable via voice chat, LOOC, or other out-of-game communication methods.
